title: Emergency Services
lede: 24 / 7 emergency response for commercial HVAC and building controls — staffed dispatch and an on-call technician roster, always available. When something fails at 2 a.m., you get a human and a truck, not a voicemail.
metaDescription: 24/7 emergency HVAC and controls response from STR Mechanical — staffed dispatch across NC, VA, and SC.
featuresHeading: Staffed dispatch. Real techs. Fast trucks.
featuresLead: Emergency response is a system, not a phone number. Ours is built on staffed dispatch, on-call rotations across all four offices, and trucks already stocked for the most common failures.

features.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"/><path d="M12 6v6l4 2"/></svg>
features.title: Guaranteed response window
features.body: PM customers get a contracted response time — typically 2-4 hours during business hours, 4-8 hours overnight. No PM contract? We still come, just behind the contract holders.

features.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M3 17l6-6 4 4 8-8"/><polyline points="14 7 21 7 21 14"/></svg>
features.title: Live dispatch, not voicemail
features.body: Calls route to a person — even at 3 a.m. on a holiday. Dispatch triages, pages the on-call tech, and stays on the line until a truck is rolling.

features.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><rect x="3" y="7" width="15" height="10" rx="1"/><circle cx="7" cy="19" r="2"/><circle cx="17" cy="19" r="2"/><path d="M18 9h3l2 4v3h-5"/></svg>
features.title: Stocked trucks
features.body: Common-failure parts onboard — contactors, capacitors, motors, belts, common refrigerants, control boards. Fewer second trips for the same call.

features.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M12 22s8-4 8-10V5l-8-3-8 3v7c0 6 8 10 8 10z"/></svg>
features.title: Mission-critical experience
features.body: Data centers, hospitals, life sciences — facilities that can't go down. We understand the protocols, the change-control, and the documentation.

features.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><polyline points="22 12 18 12 15 21 9 3 6 12 2 12"/></svg>
features.title: Temporary cooling/heating
features.body: Equipment rentals when repair time exceeds your tolerance — chillers, AHUs, spot coolers, glycol systems, electric boilers delivered and set.

features.icon: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M14 2H6a2 2 0 0 0-2 2v16a2 2 0 0 0 2 2h12a2 2 0 0 0 2-2V8z"/><polyline points="14 2 14 8 20 8"/><line x1="9" y1="15" x2="15" y2="15"/></svg>
features.title: Post-event reporting
features.body: Within 24 hours of any emergency call: root cause, parts replaced, labor hours, recommended follow-up. You get the paper trail your insurer and ownership need.

whyParagraphs: Anyone can advertise "24/7 service." Few actually staff it. Even fewer have the parts on the truck, the institutional knowledge of your building, and the relationships to expedite a part from a manufacturer at 11 p.m. on a Friday.
Four offices across NC, VA, and SC means there's almost always a truck within an hour. PM customers get priority — the contract is the queue.
whyBullets: Live dispatch, never an answering service
On-call rotation across all 4 STR offices for regional coverage
Mission-critical experience: data centers, hospitals, life sciences
Temporary equipment rentals when repair time exceeds tolerance
ctaParagraph: If something's failing in your facility right now, scroll down and call the office nearest you. If you want to set up a PM contract that puts you in the priority queue, request a proposal.
